title: LATAM Healthcare Scheduling API Infrastructure Gap
problem: LATAM healthcare providers use fragmented EMR/practice management systems with no unified scheduling API layer. Patients must call or visit to book appointments. No real-time availability across clinics. Healthcare apps can't easily integrate appointment booking. This infrastructure exists in US (Cronofy, OnSched) and Europe but is missing in LATAM.
solution: Healthcare scheduling API platform that integrates with major EMR systems in Brazil, Mexico, Colombia. Provides unified API for appointment booking, availability checking, reminders, and rescheduling. White-label solution for healthcare apps. Twilio-for-healthcare-appointments model.
technology: API-first architecture, EMR integrations, real-time availability sync, SMS/WhatsApp notifications, webhook events
target_customer: Healthcare app developers, telemedicine platforms, hospital systems, insurance companies
traction_signals: Rapid growth of healthcare apps in LATAM
WhatsApp Business API adoption in healthcare
regulatory_notes: LGPD (Brazil), LFPDPPP (Mexico), data protection in each country
varden_fit: CRITICAL FIT - This is exactly Varden's domain. Scheduling API infrastructure is core capability. Build once, sell to hundreds of healthcare apps. Infrastructure play with high margins.
notes: CONTRARIAN + BOREALING: Most boring software ever - appointment booking API. But it's essential infrastructure that every healthcare app needs. Zero competition in LATAM. Cronofy model but for 650M people.
